65.reason: [Loop bound `c <= j+1` may exceed the actual number of operators (`i`) and numbers (`j`), leading to reading uninitialized or zeroed values incorrectly. Should loop up to `j-1` or based on operator count.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-1: 0.125 , reason:  [Loop bound `j+1` may exceed valid indices of `t` and `num`, causing out-of-bounds access]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-2: 0.07692307692307693 , reason:  [Loop bound `c <= j+1` likely exceeds valid indices of `t` and `num`. `j` was over-incremented, so `j+1` may be far beyond actual number of operators (`i`) or numbers. Should loop up to `i` or `j-1`, not `j+1`.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-3: 0.125 , reason:  [Loop bound `c <= j+1` may exceed array bounds of `t` and `num`, since `j` counts numbers and `i` counts operators; `t` has size `i`, so accessing `t[c]` beyond `i-1` is invalid.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-4: 0.16666666666666666 , reason:  [Loop bound `c <= j+1` may exceed the actual number of operators (`i`) and numbers (`j`), leading to reading uninitialized or zeroed values incorrectly. Should loop up to `j-1` or based on operator count.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-5: 0.16666666666666666 , reason:  [Loop bound is too large: `j` counts numbers, so valid indices are up to `j-1`; accessing `num[c+1]` when `c` goes up to `j+1` causes out-of-bounds access. Should be `c < j` or similar.]

18.reason: [This increments `j` immediately after reading an operator, which causes misalignment between numbers and operators. For example, in "5+3", after reading '5', then '+', it moves to next number slot before reading '3'. But if there are consecutive operators or spaces, this breaks. Also, it doesn't skip non-digit/non-operator characters properly.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-2: 0.07692307692307693 , reason:  [This increments `j` immediately after reading an operator, which causes misalignment between numbers and operators. For example, in "5+3", after reading '5', then '+', it moves to next number slot before reading '3'. But if there are consecutive operators or spaces, this breaks. Also, it doesn't skip non-digit/non-operator characters properly.]
